meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 7)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Sort and classify nouns from everyday Taiwanese school life.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Kang Chiao" is a proper noun — it's the specific name of a school.

questions.question: "Boy" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "boy" refers to any boy — it's a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "September"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: September is a proper noun — month names must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "classroom" is a common noun — it refers to any classroom.

questions.question: "Linkou" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! Linkou is a proper noun — it's the name of a specific place.

questions.question: What type of noun is "classroom"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: classroom is a common noun — it refers to any classroom, not a specific named room.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: boy/classroom = general words (common). Kang Chiao/Linkou/September = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "Kang Chiao is in Linkou," both proper nouns are correctly capitalized.
questions.explanation: Yes! "Kang Chiao" (school name) and "Linkou" (place name) are both properly capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
questions.explanation: "Kang Chiao" and "Linkou" are both proper nouns — all words in a proper name must be capitalized.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Wednesday"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: It's a day of the week
questions.explanation: Wednesday is a proper noun — days of the week must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Kang Chiao is in Linkou." Both are proper nouns.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: town/playground/street = general words (common). Taipei/Pikachu = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "April" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! April is a month name — a proper noun that must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which pair shows correct common → proper noun matching?
questions.explanation: school is a common noun (lowercase). Kang Chiao is its specific proper noun form (capitalize all words).

questions.question: Fix this word: 'sosa street'. Write the correct form.
questions.hint: A specific street name is a proper noun — capitalize both words
questions.explanation: "Sosa Street" is a proper noun — both words in a specific street name must be capitalized.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Ms. Li is my teacher." Ms. Li is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which word in this sentence needs a capital letter? "we go to school every tuesday."
questions.explanation: "tuesday" should be "Tuesday" — it's a day of the week (proper noun).

questions.question: In the sentence "My pencil is yellow," the noun "pencil" should NOT be capitalized.
questions.explanation: "pencil" is a common noun — it stays lowercase (unless starting a sentence).

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: park/dog = general words (common). September/Kang Chiao/Sosa Street = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix this sentence: 'The park is on the busy street.' Is 'park' a common or proper noun?
questions.hint: Is it a specific named park?
questions.explanation: "park" (without a specific name) is a common noun — it stays lowercase.
